messages.content:
## Ground rules:
- Follow the user's instructions. 
- DO NOT wrap answers in markdown code blocks, which means no triple backticks like ``` nor single backtick like ` .
- Do not explain answers, just give me code. 
- If changing existing code, preserve unrelated code and comments. 
- Never add comments to the end of a line. 
- Never add stupid comments.
- Be considerate with indentation. 
- Prefer readable code over of comments. 
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Recent yanks (copy to clipboard):
```fish/load_last_interactive_only/audio-vido-specific.fish
_find_first_video_file_any_type
```

```fish/load_last_interactive_only/audio-vido-specific.fish
    set -l video (_find_first_video_file_any_type)
```

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Fish Shell Code Preferences

- Use snake_case for functions and variables.
- When a condition is ambiguous, assign it to a well‑named variable before the `if`.
- Prefer long options, i.e. `--quiet` over `-q`, `--regex` over `-r`
- Fish shell does not do word splitting like bash, no need to wrap everything in quotes!

## Fish syntax examples

```fish
# exploit fish-isms!

# use `not`, avoid `!`
if not test -f spoon
    echo There is no spoon
    exit 1
end

# if + set
if set -l message (some_command)
    echo "ok: $message"
else
    echo "fail: $message"
end

# prefer named arguments
function show_variable -a name value
    echo "$name = $value"
end

# fish doesn't have heredocs, use begin/end
begin
    echo $header
    echo $table
end > out.html
# or multi-line strings
echo "
foo
bar
" > test

# every variable is an array
set names wes john bob jane
for name in $names
    echo $name
end

for i in *.tmp
    if grep DONE $i
        break
    end
    if grep SKIP $i
        continue
    end
    ...
end

# combine conditions
while test -f foo.txt; or test -f bar.txt
    ...
end

# conditional execution
make; and make install; or make clean
```

### builtin fish commands

Now that we have more than 2KB of system RAM, we can fix most of what sucks about bash...

```fish
# *** string command

# match: test, filter, extract
string match --quiet '*.lua' $file
set lua_files (string match '*.lua' $files)
set version (string match --regex 'v([0-9.]+)' $tag)
string match --index --regex 'ERROR' $line

# replace: transform text
set fixed (string replace foo bar $text)
set cleaned (string replace --regex '\s+' ' ' $line)
set base (string replace --regex '\.[^.]+$' '' $file)
set normalized (string replace --filter --regex '^./' '' $paths)

# split: tokenize
set parts (string split ':' $PATH)
set lines (string split '\n' $text)
set ext (string split --right --max 1 . $file)[2]

# trim: clean edges
set clean (string trim $line)
set unquoted (string trim --chars '"' $s)

# join: build strings
string join ' ' $argv
string join --no-empty ':' $PATH

# length and slicing
string length $name
string sub --start 2 --length 4 $word
string sub --start -5 $word

# case conversion
set key (string lower $key)
set name (string upper $name)

# shell-safe encoding
set escaped (string escape $path)
string unescape $escaped

# normalize piped output
set text (some_command | string collect)


# path command
path basename foo/bar.txt # bar.txt
path basename --no-extension foo/bar.txt # bar
path dirname foo/bar.txt # foo
path extension ./foo.mp4 # .mp4
path change-extension mov ./foo.mp4 # ./foo.mov

path normalize path/to//hello.txt # path/to/hello.txt
path normalize path/to/../hello.txt # path/hello.txt
# resolve = normalize + absolute path and follow symlinks
path resolve foo  # ~/foo

path sort 10-foo 2-bar
# 2-bar
# 10-foo


# math command
math [(-s | --scale) N] [(-b | --base) BASE] [(-m | --scale-mode) MODE] EXPRESSION ...
math 10 / 6
math $status - 128
math "5 * 2"
math "sin(pi)"
math max 5,2,3,1


# count STRING1 STRING2 ...
# COMMAND | count
# count [...] < FILE
count $PATH
count *.txt
git ls-files --others --exclude-standard | count
printf '%s\n' foo bar | count baz # Returns 3 (2 lines from stdin plus 1 argument)
count < /etc/hosts # number of entries in hosts file


# contains [OPTIONS] KEY [VALUES ...]
if contains -- -q $argv
    echo '$argv contains a -q option'
end

# scripts
status filename # current running script
status basename
status dirname
status function # current function name
status line-number
status stack-trace

```

# Semantic Grep matches: 5

This is automatic context based on my request. These may or may not be relevant.
## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/misc-specific.fish:1830-1849
end
abbr --add ffi_astats_perchannel --set-cursor --function _ffi_astats_perchannel
function _ffi_astats_perchannel
    # FTR =1 results in ONLY per channel showing (one set per channel in video/audio file)
    set options "measure_perchannel=1"
    set input (_find_first_video_file_any_type; or echo _) # copy pasta
    echo -n "ffmpeg -i $input -af astats=$options% -f null -" # copy pasta
end

# IDEAS (make reusable?)... maybe just have a lookup of these in a file somewhere and grep it?
#
# * trim, slow down and label:
# ffmpeg -ss 00:00:14.5 -to 00:00:14.9 -i approve-what-dialog-plugin-mixpre6.mov -an \
#   -filter_complex "[0:v]setpts=20.0*PTS,drawtext=text='20x slow down':fontcolor=white:fontsize=200:x=1500:y=2000:box=1:boxcolor=black@0.5[v]" \
#   -map "[v]" output.mp4
#

abbr --add _aio --function abbr_aio
function abbr_aio
    echo -n "video_editing_aio "


## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/misc-specific.fish:1760-1763
function _ffp
    echo -n "ffprobe -i "
    _find_first_video_file_any_type; or echo _
end

## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/misc-specific.fish:1845-1864
#

abbr --add _aio --function abbr_aio
function abbr_aio
    echo -n "video_editing_aio "
    abbr_videos_glob_for_current_dir
end

abbr shift_only 'for i in *.mkv; video_editing_just_shift_to_mp4_one_video $i; end'
function video_editing_just_shift_to_mp4_one_video
    # TODO merge with stage_1 below which does the same thing
    # converts to mp4 + shifts by 100ms
    set video_file (realpath $argv[1])
    set output_file (path change-extension ".shifted100ms.mp4" "$video_file")
    if test -f "$output_file"
        echo "Skipping...file already exists: $output_file"
    else
        ffmpeg -i "$video_file" -itsoffset 0.1 -i "$video_file" -map 0:v -map 1:a -c:v copy -c:a aac "$output_file"
    end
end


## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/misc-specific.fish:1803-1807
function _ffi_silencedetect
    set input (_find_first_video_file_any_type; or echo _)
    # remember % is cursor placeholder
    echo -n "ffmpeg -i $input -af silencedetect=noise=d=0.1:-30dB -f null -"
end

## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/misc-specific.fish:1785-1804
abbr --add ffi_copy --set-cursor --function _ffi_copy
function _ffi_copy
    # careful w/ copy, it results in keyframe issues when trimming
    #  IIRC only want to use this when changing container... NOT when changing video
    _ffi_pass_middle_to_new_out "% -c copy"
end

abbr --add ffi_af --set-cursor --function _ffi_af
function _ffi_af
    _ffi_pass_middle_to_new_out "-af '%'"
end

abbr --add ffi_vf --set-cursor --function _ffi_vf
function _ffi_vf
    _ffi_pass_middle_to_new_out "-vf '%'"
end

abbr --add ffi_silencedetect --set-cursor --function _ffi_silencedetect
function _ffi_silencedetect
    set input (_find_first_video_file_any_type; or echo _)

turn into abbr that expands in fish sshell instead of function

Here is the code I selected:
```fish/load_last_interactive_only/audio-vido-specific.fish:64-68
function ffmpeg_volumedetect -a video_file
    set -l input (_find_first_video_file_any_type)
    ffmpeg -i $input -filter:a volumedetect -f null /dev/null 2>&1
end

```

This is not necessarily a complete selection of nearby code, this is just the part I want help with. Preserve indentation!

response_message.content:
abbr --add ffmpeg_volumedetect --function _ffmpeg_volumedetect

function _ffmpeg_volumedetect
    set -l input (_find_first_video_file_any_type)
    echo -n "ffmpeg -i $input -filter:a volumedetect -f null /dev/null 2>&1"
end
response_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to turn the function `ffmpeg_volumedetect -a video_file` into an abbreviation that expands in fish shell instead of a function. The code:

```
function ffmpeg_volumedetect -a video_file
    set -l input (_find_first_video_file_any_type)
    ffmpeg -i $input -filter:a volumedetect -f null /dev/null 2>&1
end
```

We need to convert this to an `abbr` that expands to the command. The original function takes an argument `video_file` but actually ignores it and uses `_find_first_video_file_any_type`. Probably they want an abbreviation that runs the ffmpeg volumedetect on the first video file.

In fish, `abbr` can have `--function` to expand to a function? Actually abbreviations can be plain text expansion. They can also run a function: `abbr --add name --function func`. The user wants "abbr that expands in fish shell instead of function". So they likely want something like:

```
abbr --add ffmpeg_volumedetect --set-cursor --function _ffmpeg_volumedetect
function _ffmpeg_volumedetect
    set -l input (_find_first_video_file_any_type)
    echo -n "ffmpeg -i $input -filter:a volumedetect -f null /dev/null 2>&1"
end
```

But they asked "turn into abbr that expands in fish sshell instead of function". Maybe they want the abbreviation to expand to the full command (including execution) when typed. Usually `abbr` expands text in the command line, not execute automatically. Could use `abbr --add name --function` which expands to the output of the function? Actually fish's abbreviation system: `abbr --add name --function fn` expands to the result of the function? I think `abbr --add name --function fn` will replace the abbreviation with the result of running function `fn`. The function should output the text to be inserted.

Thus we can replace the function with an abbreviation that calls `_ffmpeg_volumedetect` that echoes the command. Or we could define the abbreviation directly with a function inline? The user wants preserve indentation. So we need to remove the function definition and replace with abbr.

Implementation:

```
abbr --add ffmpeg_volumedetect --function _ffmpeg_volumedetect

function _ffmpeg_volumedetect
    set -l input (_find_first_video_file_any_type)
    echo -n "ffmpeg -i $input -filter:a volumedetect -f null /dev/null 2>&1"
end
```

But also preserve any flags like `--set-cursor` perhaps. In earlier examples they used `--set-cursor` for abbreviations that include `%` placeholder. Not needed here.

Thus answer: code replacing function with abbreviation and helper function.

We should output only code, no markdown.
